prompt: Create a square abstract expressionist painting with no text, dominated by vivid golden yellow and deep teal green. The composition should feel like a turbulent river of paint: a thick, bright yellow S-shaped stream begins at the lower center, curves upward through the middle, and explodes into a wide swirling splash on the left side before curling toward a large circular vortex near the upper center-right. Use layered acrylic and mixed-media textures with palette-knife ridges, rough scraped paint, speckles, splatters, transparent washes, and visible canvas grain. Include thin looping arc lines around the upper vortex, horizontal scraped streaks across the midline, vertical paint drips especially on the right side, and scattered small yellow droplets across the lower half. Keep the edges painterly and imperfect, with dark emerald shadows and lighter mint-green patches adding depth. The mood should be energetic, fluid, raw, and contemporary, like a high-resolution gallery photograph of an abstract canvas, with balanced contrast and rich tactile surface detail.
